body: PT Multi Bintang Indonesia Tbk (MLBI) reported a 28.6% year-on-year increase in net profit to IDR 762.57 billion in the first nine months of 2024, driven by a 2.5% increase in net sales to IDR 2.33 trillion. The company's profit growth was also supported by cost-saving measures, including a 1.2% decrease in cost of goods sold, a 12% decrease in selling expenses, and a 1.17% decrease in general administrative expenses.
title: PT Multi Bintang Indonesia's net profit surges 28.6% to IDR 762.57 billion in 9M 2024.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) fell to 7,761.41, down from 7,783.15 at the opening bell, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESG also declining. While big names like Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BBRI) and Bank Mandiri (BMRI) saw corrections, other blue-chip stocks like Bank Central Asia (BBCA) and Indofood Sukses Makmur (INDF) experienced positive shifts.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Falls in Morning Session Amid Muted Market Response to New Cabinet Inauguration

body: PT Unilever Indonesia Tbk (UNVR) reported a net profit of IDR 3.01 trillion for the first nine months of 2024, a 28.1% year-on-year decline from IDR 4.19 trillion in the same period last year, due to lower sales of IDR 27.42 trillion. The company's sales were led by household and body care products, which contributed IDR 17.59 trillion, followed by food and beverages with IDR 9.82 trillion.
title: Unilever Indonesia's net profit drops 28.1% to IDR 3.01 trillion in the first nine months of 2024.

body: PT Gudang Garam Tbk (GGRM) has injected IDR 300 billion into PT Surya Dhoho Investama (SDHI) through the issuance of 300,000 new shares, worth IDR 1 million each, increasing SDHI's paid-up capital to IDR 14.3 trillion from IDR 14 trillion. The capital injection aims to support the operational activities of Dhoho Kediri Airport in East Java, established through SDHI, with GGRM holding total assets of IDR 87.75 trillion and total equity of IDR 61.79 trillion as of June 30, 2024.
title: Gudang Garam injects IDR 300 billion into Surya Dhoho Investama to boost capital for operational activities.

body: PT Triputra Agro Persada Tbk (TAPG) reported a 46.6% year-over-year surge in profit in Q3 2024, driven by higher revenue and better efficiency, particularly in finance expenses, with revenue reaching IDR 6.24 trillion, up 3.37% from IDR 6.03 trillion in Q3 2023. The company's gross profit, operating profit, and profit before taxes also increased significantly, by 33.02%, 67.46%, and 50.93%, respectively, due to cost efficiency and a reduction in finance expenses deficit.
title: PT Triputra Agro Persada's Profit Surges 46.6% in Q3 2024 on Higher Revenue and Improved Efficiency.

body: PT Petrosea Tbk (PTRO) has formed a new subsidiary, PT Petrosea Infrastruktur Nusantara (PIN), to support business expansion, with PTRO holding 99.90% of its shares. PIN is currently conducting due diligence on acquiring several mining infrastructure assets, aligning with PTRO's plan to expand and diversify its mining service contracts, following a recent investment allocation of USD 400 million in new mining equipment.
title: Petrosea forms new subsidiary to support business expansion and diversification into infrastructure management and mining service contracts.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) reached a new high of 7,800.02 before weakening to 7,778.44 at 11:30 am, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL also declining. Meanwhile, some stocks such as PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk, PT Bank Central Asia Tbk, and PT Indofood Sukses Makmur Tbk gained, while others like PT Mulia Boga Raya Tbk, PT Unilever Indonesia Tbk, and PT Kimia Farma Tbk were corrected.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Reaches New High Before Weakening in Morning Session

body: PT Bakrie & Brothers Tbk (BNBR) is planning a capital increase of 13.35 billion shares, equivalent to 7.70% of its issued and paid-up capital, to settle debts worth IDR 855 billion owed to creditors, including Eurofa Smil, at a conversion rate of IDR 64 per share. The company will seek approval from its next general meeting of shareholders on November 28, 2024, for this corporate action.
title: Bakrie & Brothers to issue 13.35 billion new shares to settle IDR 855 billion debt with creditors.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) closed at 7,789.07, up from 7,764.73, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL also experiencing a slight upturn. Pharmaceutical and healthcare companies, including PT Kalbe Farma Tbk (KLBF), PT Tempo Scan Pacific Tbk (TPCP), and PT Siloam International Hospitals Tbk (SILO), saw gains, while others in the same industry, such as PT Industri Jamu dan Farmasi Sido Muncul Tbk (SIDO) and PT Kimia Farma Tbk (KAEF), experienced corrections.
title: Indonesian Stock Market Closes Higher with Composite Index Reaching 7,789.07

body: PT Lingkar Matra acquired 20.39% shares of PT Communication Cable System Indonesia Tbk (CCSI) through PT Pluang Maju Sekuritas, while PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk (TRIM) increased its share portion in PT Energi Mega Persada Tbk (ENRG) to 14.41% by purchasing 353.80 million shares. Other notable transactions include PT Karya Nusa Perdana's acquisition of 10 million shares of PT Maxindo Karya Anugerah Tbk (MKAY), and UOB Kay Hian Ltd's sale of 33.77 million shares of PT Golden Flower Tbk (GFLO).
title: Multiple investors acquire and sell shares in various Indonesian companies across different industries.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net volume of 223.65 million shares on Friday, with top buys including PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 229.18 million shares, PT J Resources Pacific Tbk with 108.28 million shares, and PT Multipolar Tbk with 63.62 million shares. Meanwhile, top sells included PT Bumi Resources Tbk with -523.81 million shares, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk with -39.62 million shares, and PT Bukalapak.com Tbk with -21.94 million shares.
title: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market purchase majority shares in digital and retail companies, sell majority shares in coal mining and banking companies.

body: Credit distributions in Indonesia are expected to rise 88.3% in Q4 2024 compared to Q3 2024, with loan growth prioritizing working capital, investment, and consumption credits, accompanied by stricter credit distribution policies. Meanwhile, third-party funds collection is projected to increase 89.3% in Q4 2024, with all instruments, including savings, deposits, and current accounts, following the same trend.
title: Bank Indonesia Projects Credit Distribution and Loan Growth to Increase in Q4 2024
